mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching economic contraction, prompting risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 prices over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing core inflation indicates expanding profit margins and healthy demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The spread between quarterly GDPC1 growth and monthly CPIAUCSL inflation shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05 over 3 years).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 60-day forward SPX returns, or the relationship reverses during supply-constrained environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals collapsing consumer confidence and credit conditions, triggering immediate risk aversion that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances where housing starts drop below 1100 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns more frequently than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth sustains consumer spending power, which underpins corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over 5 years).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs suppress corporate leverage and equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).
